title: Pixelsonly Circuit Record
description: Canonical schema for one motorsport circuit. Designed for the most demanding consumer (the apex site circuit pages): per-corner records and layout variants are first-class. The poster workflow reads a subset of the same record. One source, multiple consumers.

slug.description: URL key and folder name. Permanent once published. Must match the containing folder name.

name.description: Full official current name, including sponsor (e.g. 'WeatherTech Raceway Laguna Seca').

display_name.description: Short name for the poster headline (e.g. 'Laguna Seca').

subtitle.description: Optional secondary line under the poster headline.

country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2. Drives the flag asset.

locality.description: Location line on the poster face (e.g. 'Salinas, California, USA').

latitude.description: Circuit center, decimal degrees. Verify against an authoritative source, not a single map pin.

primary_layout.description: id of the layout used as the default for poster + circuit-page rendering. Must reference an existing entry in layouts[].

layouts.description: Each configuration of the circuit, addressable independently. Single-layout tracks have exactly one entry. Multi-layout tracks (Nordschleife, pre/post-redesign Spa, GP/short) get one entry each.

sources.description: Record-level provenance. At least one authoritative source is REQUIRED. Source citation is not optional in this repo.

status.description: draft = populated, not yet cross-checked. verified = cross-checked against >=2 sources. canonical = signed off for print + production.

id.description: Layout key, unique within this circuit (e.g. 'grand-prix', 'nordschleife', 'pre-2007').

name.description: Human label for the layout (e.g. 'Grand Prix', 'Nordschleife').

current.description: Whether this layout is in current use. Historical/retired layouts set false.

years.description: Optional. Period this layout was/is in use (e.g. '1957-present', '1979-2006').

map_svg.description: Filename (relative to the track folder) of the simplified single-line outline SVG for THIS layout.

corners.description: Per-corner records as first-class data, not buried in narrative.

id.description: Corner key, unique within the layout.

number.description: Turn number(s) as displayed (string to allow compound turns like '8-8A').

description.description: Short factual description of the corner.

story.description: Optional longer narrative / notable moments at this corner.

position.description: Optional normalized position on the layout's map viewBox (0-1, origin top-left), so the apex page can label the corner on the map.

record_type.description: Optional. Whether this is an official race lap, qualifying, or unofficial outright record.

sources.description: Lap records REQUIRE a source citation.

editorial.description: Website + Shopify copy. Not printed on the poster face.

narrative_md.description: Filename (relative to the track folder) of the long-form narrative Markdown file.

card_url.description: Short/trackable URL encoded on the companion card's QR + NFC (301-redirects to the circuit page).

flag_svg.description: Filename (relative to the track folder) of the country flag SVG. Carries its source license (see LICENSE-ASSETS.md).

map_source.description: Optional. Filename (relative to the track folder) of the original, un-simplified SVG that the layout's map_svg was derived from (e.g. the Wikimedia Commons original, committed as 'map-source.svg' and SVGO-optimized for the repo — visually identical to upstream, editor metadata stripped). Present whenever the production map was traced/extracted from a third-party geometry source. The website may render this richer original; its license + credit live in map_attribution and LICENSE-ASSETS.md.

mapAttribution.description: Provenance + license for the track map, surfaced as a credit line on the apex circuit page and consumable by downstream tools. Present when map.svg (and/or the committed map_source) is derived from a third-party source such as Wikimedia Commons. Richer than satellite.attribution (a single string) because the map credit is rendered, not just legally required. LICENSE-ASSETS.md carries the canonical register row; this block is the machine-readable mirror so consumers never have to scrape it.

source_url.description: Canonical URL of the source work's page (e.g. its Wikimedia Commons file page).

license.description: License short name as published by the source (e.g. 'CC BY-SA 3.0', 'Public domain').

license_url.description: Optional. URL of the license deed (e.g. 'https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0').

artist.description: Optional. Author/creator credit as published by the source.

attribution_text.description: Optional. Ready-to-render one-line credit string, composed from the fields above so consumers can display it verbatim without reassembling parts.

accessed.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the source was fetched.

satellite.description: The raw Sentinel-2 master lives in a PRIVATE R2 bucket and is never published. Only the stylized derivative is public.

derivative.description: Filename (relative to the track folder) of the published, stylized/cropped image used on poster + web.

source_key.description: Informational only: the private R2 key of the raw master (e.g. 'tracks/laguna-seca/laguna-seca.tiff'). Not a public asset.

attribution.description: Required wherever the derivative is shown/sold, e.g. 'Contains modified Copernicus Sentinel data 2026'.

accessed.description: ISO date the source was accessed/verified (YYYY-MM-DD).
